questions.text: The point of intersection of the horizontal and vertical axes is called:
questions.explanation: The origin is the starting point (0,0) where the x and y axes meet.

questions.text: The horizontal line in a Cartesian plane is called the:
questions.explanation: The x-axis is horizontal, while the y-axis is vertical.

questions.text: The coordinates of the origin are:
questions.explanation: At the origin, both x and y distances are zero.

questions.text: A point both of whose coordinates are negative lies in:
questions.explanation: In Quadrant III, x < 0 and y < 0.

questions.text: The abscissa of any point on the y-axis is:
questions.explanation: Any point on the y-axis has no horizontal displacement, so x = 0.

questions.text: The ordinate of any point on the x-axis is:
questions.explanation: Any point on the x-axis has no vertical displacement, so y = 0.

questions.text: Points (-3, 5) lies in the:
questions.explanation: Here x is negative and y is positive, which corresponds to the second quadrant.

questions.text: The x-coordinate of a point is also called its:
questions.explanation: Abscissa refers to the x-value, ordinate refers to the y-value.

questions.text: If the y-coordinate of a point is zero, then this point always lies:
questions.explanation: When y=0, the point is located exactly on the horizontal axis.

questions.text: The perpendicular distance of the point P(3, 4) from the y-axis is:
questions.explanation: Distance from y-axis is given by the absolute value of the x-coordinate (abscissa).

questions.text: The perpendicular distance of the point P(4, 3) from the x-axis is:
questions.explanation: Distance from x-axis is given by the absolute value of the y-coordinate (ordinate).

questions.text: Abscissa of a point is positive in:
questions.explanation: x is positive on the right side of the y-axis (Quadrants I and IV).

questions.text: The point (0, -7) lies:
questions.explanation: Since the x-coordinate is 0, the point must lie on the vertical axis (y-axis).

questions.text: In which quadrant do points with signs (+, -) lie?
questions.explanation: Positive x and negative y describe the bottom-right section (IV).

questions.text: Ordinate of a point is negative in:
questions.explanation: y is negative below the x-axis (Quadrants III and IV).

questions.text: The axes divide the Cartesian plane into how many parts?
questions.explanation: The plane is divided into four quadrants.

questions.text: The point (-10, 0) lies:
questions.explanation: Since y=0 and x is negative, it is on the negative part of the horizontal axis.

questions.text: If a point is at a distance of 5 units from the x-axis and 2 units from the y-axis in the IV quadrant, its coordinates are:
questions.explanation: Distance from y-axis = x = 2. Distance from x-axis = |y| = 5. In IV quadrant, y is negative, so (2, -5).

questions.text: Which point lies on the x-axis?
questions.explanation: Points on the x-axis must have y = 0.

questions.text: The point whose ordinate is 4 and which lies on y-axis is:
questions.explanation: Lying on the y-axis means x = 0. Ordinate is the y-value.

questions.text: Points (1, -1), (2, -2), (4, -5), (-3, -4) - which does not lie in the IV quadrant?
questions.explanation: (-3, -4) has both coordinates negative, so it lies in the III quadrant.

questions.text: The distance of the point (h, k) from the origin is:
questions.explanation: By Pythagoras theorem, distance d = √(x² + y²).

questions.text: If the coordinates of a point are (0, 0), then the point is:
questions.explanation: The origin (0,0) lies on both axes simultaneously.

questions.text: A point whose abscissa is -3 and ordinate is 2 lies in:
questions.explanation: x < 0 and y > 0 is the Second Quadrant.

questions.text: Signs of the coordinates of a point in the II quadrant are:
questions.explanation: In Quadrant II, x is negative (left) and y is positive (up).

questions.text: The ordinate of every point on the x-axis is:
questions.explanation: On the x-axis, there is no vertical movement, so y = 0.

questions.text: The name of the system used for locating a point in a plane is:
questions.explanation: It is commonly called the Cartesian coordinate system.

questions.text: If P(-1, 1), Q(3, -4), R(1, -1), S(-2, -3) and T(-4, 4) are plotted, which points lie in the IV quadrant?
questions.explanation: Q(3, -4) and R(1, -1) have positive x and negative y.

questions.text: Point (-4, -4) is equidistant from:
questions.explanation: The distances are |-4| = 4 and |-4| = 4, so it is equidistant from both axes.

questions.text: A point lies on the y-axis at a distance of 5 units in the negative direction. Its coordinates are:
questions.explanation: On y-axis means x = 0. Negative direction 5 units means y = -5.

questions.text: The Cartesian plane is also called:
questions.explanation: These are common synonyms in geometry.

questions.text: Abscissa of (5, 2) is:
questions.explanation: Abscissa is the x-coordinate.

questions.text: Ordinate of (2, 5) is:
questions.explanation: Ordinate is the y-coordinate.

questions.text: Point (x, y) where x > 0 and y > 0 is in quadrant:
questions.explanation: Both positive coordinates define the first quadrant.

questions.text: Point (x, y) where x < 0 and y < 0 is in quadrant:
questions.explanation: Both negative coordinates define the third quadrant.

questions.text: The x-axis and y-axis are ______ to each other.
questions.explanation: They meet at a 90-degree angle at the origin.

questions.text: The distance of point (3, 0) from the origin is:
questions.explanation: Distance = √(3² + 0²) = 3.

questions.text: Point (10, -10) lies in quadrant:
questions.explanation: x positive, y negative is quadrant IV.

questions.text: To plot the point (4, 5), we move 4 units along ______ and 5 units along ______.
questions.explanation: First coordinate is for x (horizontal), second is for y (vertical).

questions.text: René Descartes is credited with the development of:
questions.explanation: Cartesian geometry is named after him.
